page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d3c.14xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. The instance features 56 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. It is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

The instance is configured with 224.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key characteristic of this server type is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 54975 GB of local HDD storage. This configuration prioritizes high-capacity local storage density over SSD-tier random access speeds.

For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators. The primary hardware tradeoff lies in the storage medium; while the local storage capacity is exceptionally large, the HDD technology limits sequential and random I/O performance compared to NVMe SSDs.

Given the massive local HDD capacity and balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for storage-intensive workloads that require large-scale data retention without the need for high-IOPS SSD performance. Suitable workloads include big data analytics, distributed file systems, data warehousing, and cold storage archiving. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the use of HDD storage instead of SSDs allows for a lower cost per gigabyte of local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d3c.14xlarge is an x86_64 server instance running on the KVM hypervisor, equipped with an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. It features 56 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.9 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading. The system includes 224.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. A defining feature of this instance is its massive 54975 GB of local HDD storage, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are provided. The primary hardware tradeoff is the reliance on high-capacity HDD storage rather than high-speed SSDs, making it highly cost-efficient for bulk data storage. This instance is optimized for storage-heavy workloads such as big data processing, distributed file systems, and large-scale data warehousing.
